title: Abu al-Jud
body: Abū al-Jūd Muḥammad b. Aḥmad b. al-Layth Persian: ابو جود محمد بن احمد بن لیث was an Iranian mathematician who lived during 10th century and was a contemporary of al-Biruni. He used conics to solve quartic and cubic equations, a century before the more famous work of Omar Khayyam, although his solution did not deal with all the cases.
